title: hopea cordata
intro: Hopea cordata, also known as the Balau tree or Belian tree, is a species of tree that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The extracts of this tree have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Hopea cordata are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
